96	declarations below formalise steps (2)–(3) — assembling those two families into a global
97	presentation and feeding it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` — turning what were
98	prose steps in the Handoff into axiom-clean Lean.  The single remaining mathematical input is the
99	affine global-generation theorem (step (1)), which supplies the two `GeneratingSections`.
100	-/
101	
102	/-- A finite-free / free `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module is quasi-coherent: it is the tilde of `ι →₀ R`
103	(`tildeFinsupp`), and quasi-coherence is closed under isomorphism.  Project-local supplement; used
104	to recognise the kernel-side coefficient sheaf of the 01I8 presentation route as quasi-coherent. -/
105	instance free_isQuasicoherent (ι : Type u) :
106	    (SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) ι).IsQuasicoherent :=
107	  (SheafOfModules.isQuasicoherent.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).prop_of_iso
108	    (tildeFinsupp (R := R) ι) inferInstance
109	
110	/-- **01I8 steps (2)–(3), packaged.**  If an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is globally generated
111	(`σ : F.GeneratingSections`, a global epimorphism `free σ.I ⟶ F`) and the kernel of that
112	epimorphism is itself globally generated (`τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections`), then the
113	tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` is an isomorphism.  This bundles the two generating families
114	into a global `F.Presentation` and feeds it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`; it is
115	the formal content of steps (2)–(3) of the 01I8 decomposition.  The single remaining mathematical
116	input is the affine global-generation theorem (step (1)) producing `σ` and `τ` for a quasi-coherent
117	`F`.  Project-local because it repackages the Mathlib presentation criterion in the
118	two-generating-families form the 01I8 route consumes. -/
119	lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
120	    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
121	    IsIso F.fromTildeΓ := by
122	  have P : F.Presentation := { generators := σ, relations := τ }
123	  exact isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
124	
125	/-- **Affine structure theorem from global generation (Stacks 01HV/01I8).**  An `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module
126	`F` that is globally generated (`σ`) together with a globally generated kernel of the generating
127	epimorphism (`τ`) is isomorphic to the sheaf associated with its module of global sections
128	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  Discharges the `IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` hypothesis of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
129	via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections`.  Project-local for the same packaging reason; once the
130	affine global-generation theorem supplies `σ`/`τ` for quasi-coherent `F` the named
131	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` upgrades to the unconditional statement. -/
132	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
133	    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
134	    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
135	  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections F σ τ
136	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm

138	/-! ### Route P, step 0 — finite trivialising standard cover
139	
140	The pure-topology brick of the global-generation route: any open cover of an affine
141	`Spec R` refines to a *finite* cover by basic opens, each contained in a cover member,
142	with the defining elements generating the unit ideal.  This is the common prerequisite
143	of the localisation-of-sections and global-generation steps. -/
144	
145	/-- **Finite basic-open refinement of a cover of `Spec R` (Stacks 01I8, topology brick).**
146	Given a family of opens `U : ι → (Spec R).Opens` covering the whole space
147	(`⨆ i, U i = ⊤`), there are finitely many elements `f : Fin n → R` and indices
148	`φ : Fin n → ι` such that each basic open `D(f j)` lies inside `U (φ j)` and the `f j`
149	generate the unit ideal (equivalently the `D(f j)` already cover `Spec R`).  Project-local
150	because it packages the basis-refinement + quasicompactness of `Spec R` in the exact form
151	the Route-P localisation/global-generation lanes consume. -/
152	lemma exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover {ι : Type*} (U : ι → (Spec R).Opens)
153	    (hU : ⨆ i, U i = ⊤) :
154	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → ι),
155	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (f j) ≤ U (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range f) = ⊤ := by
156	  classical
157	  -- pointwise: each `x` lies in a basic open contained in some cover member
158	  have hpt : ∀ x : PrimeSpectrum R, ∃ (g : R) (i : ι),
159	      x ∈ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ∧ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ≤ U i := by
160	    intro x
161	    have hxtop : x ∈ (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens) := trivial
162	    rw [← hU] at hxtop
163	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.1 hxtop
164	    obtain ⟨V, hV, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
165	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.1
166	        (PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens (R := R))) hi
167	    obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := hV
168	    exact ⟨g, i, hxV, hVU⟩
169	  choose g φ' hxg hgU using hpt
170	  -- quasicompactness: extract a finite subcover of the pointwise basic opens
171	  have hcover : (Set.univ : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) ⊆
172	      ⋃ x, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) :=
173	    fun x _ => Set.mem_iUnion.2 ⟨x, hxg x⟩
174	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := isCompact_univ.elim_finite_subcover
175	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)))
176	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x)).isOpen) hcover
177	  set e := t.equivFin with he
178	  refine ⟨t.card, fun j => g (e.symm j).val, fun j => φ' (e.symm j).val, fun j => hgU _, ?_⟩
179	  -- the chosen finite family of basic opens already covers `Spec R`
180	  rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff, eq_top_iff]
181	  intro x _
182	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
183	  have hxu := ht (Set.mem_univ x)
184	  rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at hxu
185	  obtain ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩ := hxu
186	  refine ⟨e ⟨y, hy⟩, ?_⟩
187	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
188	  exact hxy
189	
190	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `IsLocalizedModule` is local on a finite spanning cover
191	
192	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover` (Stacks 01I8, P1b): the pure commutative-algebra patching
193	primitive feeding the localisation-of-sections step.  If an `R`-linear map `g : M → N` becomes a
194	localisation at the powers of `f` after localising at the powers of each member `s j` of a finite
195	unit-ideal-spanning family, then `g` is itself a localisation at the powers of `f`.  Proved directly
196	from the three defining clauses of `IsLocalizedModule` by descent along the spanning cover (the
197	partition-of-unity argument of the blueprint).  Project-local: Mathlib has the analogous
198	`Module.Finite`/`Module.FinitePresentation` span-descent lemmas but not this one for the
199	`IsLocalizedModule` predicate itself.
200	-/
201	
202	section SpanCoverLocalization
203	
204	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {M N : Type*}
205	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
206	
207	/-- Partition of unity for a finite unit-ideal-spanning family raised to a uniform power:
208	if `s : Fin n → R` spans the unit ideal then so do the `A`-th powers, giving coefficients
209	`c` with `∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A = 1`.  Project-local helper for `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
210	private lemma exists_sum_pow_eq_one {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
211	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (A : ℕ) :
212	    ∃ c : Fin n → R, ∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A = 1 := by
213	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range fun j => (s j) ^ A) = ⊤ := by
214	    have h := Ideal.span_pow_eq_top (Set.range s) hs A
215	    rwa [← Set.range_comp] at h
216	  have h1 : (1 : R) ∈ Ideal.span (Set.range fun j => (s j) ^ A) := by rw [hspan]; trivial
217	  rw [Ideal.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun] at h1
218	  exact h1
219	
220	/-- Span-cover descent for membership in the range of a linear map: if `(s j) ^ A • w` lies in the
221	range of `g` for every member of a unit-ideal-spanning family, then `w` itself does.  Project-local
222	helper for the surjectivity clause of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
223	private lemma mem_range_of_span_pow {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
224	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (A : ℕ) (w : N)
225	    (hj : ∀ j, ∃ m : M, (s j) ^ A • w = g m) : ∃ m : M, w = g m := by
226	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := exists_sum_pow_eq_one s hs A
227	  choose m hm using hj
228	  refine ⟨∑ j, c j • m j, ?_⟩
229	  rw [map_sum]
230	  calc w = (∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [hc, one_smul]
231	    _ = ∑ j, (c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [Finset.sum_smul]
232	    _ = ∑ j, c j • ((s j) ^ A • w) := by simp_rw [mul_smul]
233	    _ = ∑ j, c j • g (m j) := by simp_rw [hm]
234	    _ = ∑ j, g (c j • m j) := by simp_rw [map_smul]
235	
236	/-- Span-cover descent for vanishing: if `(s j) ^ A • w = 0` for every member of a
237	unit-ideal-spanning family, then `w = 0`.  Project-local helper for the equaliser clause of
238	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
239	private lemma eq_zero_of_span_pow {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
240	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (A : ℕ) (w : N)
241	    (hj : ∀ j, (s j) ^ A • w = 0) : w = 0 := by
242	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := exists_sum_pow_eq_one s hs A
243	  calc w = (∑ j, c j * (s j) ^ A) • w := by rw [hc, one_smul]
244	    _ = ∑ j, c j • ((s j) ^ A • w) := by rw [Finset.sum_smul]; simp_rw [mul_smul]
245	    _ = 0 := by simp_rw [hj, smul_zero, Finset.sum_const_zero]
246	
247	/-- Localising the multiplication-by-`c` endomorphism of `N` at `S` is multiplication by `c` on the
248	localised module (as underlying functions).  Project-local helper for the `map_units` clause of
249	`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
250	private lemma map_smul_endFun (S : Submonoid R) (c : R) :
251	    ⇑(LocalizedModule.map S (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) c))
252	      = ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S N)) c) := by
253	  funext x
254	  induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
255	  | _ m t =>
256	    rw [LocalizedModule.map_mk]
257	    simp [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk]
258	
259	/-- Arithmetic of "bumping" two scalar powers up to uniform exponents.  Project-local helper for the
260	surjectivity/equaliser clauses of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
261	private lemma bump_eq {P : Type*} [AddCommGroup P] [Module R P] (c d : R) (y : P)
262	    {a k A K : ℕ} (ha : a ≤ A) (hk : k ≤ K) :
263	    c ^ A • d ^ K • y = c ^ (A - a) • d ^ (K - k) • (c ^ a • d ^ k • y) := by
264	  simp only [smul_smul]
265	  congr 1
266	  have hc : c ^ A = c ^ (A - a) * c ^ a := by rw [← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel ha]
267	  have hd : d ^ K = d ^ (K - k) * d ^ k := by rw [← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel hk]
268	  rw [hc, hd]; ring
269	
270	/-- Per-cover-member surjectivity datum: from the hypothesis that the `(s j)`-localised map is a
271	localisation at the powers of `f`, every `y : N` is hit by `g` up to a power of `s j` and a power of
272	`f`.  Project-local helper for the surjectivity clause of `isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`. -/
273	private lemma per_j_surj (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) (c : R)
274	    (hj : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
275	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
276	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
277	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g))
278	    (y : N) : ∃ (a k : ℕ) (m : M), c ^ a • f ^ k • y = g m := by
279	  haveI := hj
280	  obtain ⟨p, hxj⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers f)
281	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
282	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
283	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g)
284	      (LocalizedModule.mk y 1)
285	  obtain ⟨xj, ⟨tf, kk, (rfl : f ^ kk = tf)⟩⟩ := p
286	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk] at hxj
287	  revert hxj
288	  induction xj using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
289	  | _ m u =>
290	    intro hxj
291	    rw [IsLocalizedModule.map_LocalizedModules] at hxj
292	    obtain ⟨⟨u', uu, (rfl : c ^ uu = u')⟩, hu'⟩ := (LocalizedModule.mk_eq).1 hxj
293	    obtain ⟨u2, vv, (rfl : c ^ vv = u2)⟩ := u
294	    simp only [Submonoid.smul_def, one_smul] at hu'
295	    refine ⟨vv + uu, kk, c ^ uu • m, ?_⟩
296	    rw [map_smul]
297	    rw [show c ^ (vv + uu) • f ^ kk • y = c ^ uu • c ^ vv • (f ^ kk • y) by
298	          rw [pow_add]; simp only [smul_smul]; ring_nf]
299	    exact hu'
300	
301	/-- Per-cover-member equaliser datum: from the hypothesis that the `(s j)`-localised map is a
302	localisation at the powers of `f`, any `z` with `g z = 0` is annihilated by a power of `s j` times a
303	power of `f`.  Project-local helper for the equaliser clause. -/
304	private lemma per_j_eq (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) (c : R)
305	    (hj : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
306	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
307	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
308	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g))
309	    (z : M) (hz : g z = 0) : ∃ (a k : ℕ), c ^ a • f ^ k • z = 0 := by
310	  haveI := hj
311	  have key : (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
312	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
313	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g) (LocalizedModule.mk z 1)
314	      = (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers c)
315	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) M)
316	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers c) N) g) 0 := by
317	    rw [map_zero, IsLocalizedModule.map_LocalizedModules, hz, LocalizedModule.zero_mk]
318	  obtain ⟨⟨cc, kk, (rfl : f ^ kk = cc)⟩, hcc⟩ := hj.exists_of_eq key
319	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk, smul_zero] at hcc
320	  rw [← LocalizedModule.zero_mk (1 : Submonoid.powers c), LocalizedModule.mk_eq] at hcc
321	  obtain ⟨⟨u, aa, (rfl : c ^ aa = u)⟩, hu⟩ := hcc
322	  simp only [Submonoid.smul_def, one_smul, smul_zero] at hu
323	  exact ⟨aa, kk, hu⟩
324	
325	/-- **`IsLocalizedModule` is local on a finite spanning cover (Stacks 01I8, P1b).**  If an
326	`R`-linear map `g : M → N` becomes a localisation at the powers of `f` after localising at the
327	powers of each member `s j` of a finite family spanning the unit ideal, then `g` is itself a
328	localisation at the powers of `f`.  Proved by descent of the three defining clauses of
329	`IsLocalizedModule` along the spanning cover (the partition-of-unity argument).  Project-local:
330	Mathlib has analogous span-descent lemmas for `Module.Finite`/`Module.FinitePresentation` but not
331	for the `IsLocalizedModule` predicate itself. -/
332	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover
333	    (g : M →ₗ[R] N) (f : R) {n : ℕ} (s : Fin n → R)
334	    (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
335	    (h : ∀ j, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
336	      (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (s j))
337	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s j)) M)
338	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s j)) N) g)) :
339	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := by
340	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
341	  · -- `f` acts invertibly on `N`
342	    intro x
343	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := x.2
344	    rw [show ((x : R)) = f ^ k from hk.symm, map_pow]
345	    apply IsUnit.pow
346	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
347	    apply bijective_of_localized_span (Set.range s) hs
348	    rintro ⟨r, j, rfl⟩
349	    rw [show ⇑(LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (s j)) (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) f))
350	        = ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s j)) N)) f)
351	        from map_smul_endFun _ _, ← Module.End.isUnit_iff]
352	    exact (h j).map_units ⟨f, 1, by simp⟩
353	  · -- every `y : N` is hit by `g` up to a power of `f`
354	    intro y
355	    choose a k m hm using fun j => per_j_surj g f (s j) (h j) y
356	    set K := Finset.univ.sup k
357	    set A := Finset.univ.sup a
358	    have hw : ∀ j, ∃ mm : M, (s j) ^ A • (f ^ K • y) = g mm := by
359	      intro j
360	      have ha : a j ≤ A := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
361	      have hkk : k j ≤ K := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
362	      refine ⟨(s j) ^ (A - a j) • f ^ (K - k j) • m j, ?_⟩
363	      rw [bump_eq (s j) f y ha hkk, hm j, map_smul, map_smul]
364	    obtain ⟨mm, hmm⟩ := mem_range_of_span_pow s hs g A (f ^ K • y) hw
365	    exact ⟨⟨mm, ⟨f ^ K, K, rfl⟩⟩, hmm⟩
366	  · -- `g`-equal elements agree up to a power of `f`
367	    intro x₁ x₂ he
368	    have hgz : g (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by rw [map_sub, he, sub_self]
369	    choose a k hk using fun j => per_j_eq g f (s j) (h j) (x₁ - x₂) hgz
370	    set K := Finset.univ.sup k
371	    set A := Finset.univ.sup a
372	    have hw : ∀ j, (s j) ^ A • (f ^ K • (x₁ - x₂)) = 0 := by
373	      intro j
374	      have ha : a j ≤ A := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
375	      have hkk : k j ≤ K := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ j)
376	      rw [bump_eq (s j) f (x₁ - x₂) ha hkk, hk j, smul_zero, smul_zero]
377	    have hzero : f ^ K • (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := eq_zero_of_span_pow s hs A _ hw
378	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ K, K, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
379	    rw [← sub_eq_zero, ← smul_sub]
380	    exact hzero
381	
382	end SpanCoverLocalization
383	
384	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B local model: section restriction localizes
385	
386	The Route B keystone (`qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`) asserts that for a *quasi-coherent*
387	`F` the section-restriction `Γ(Spec R, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` exhibits the target as the localization
388	of the source at the powers of `f`.  The two declarations here are the **local model** of that
389	statement — the case where `F` is already (isomorphic to) the associated sheaf `M^~` of an
390	`R`-module.  This is the load-bearing brick the keystone descends over its trivialising cover: on
391	each piece `D(g_j)` of a finite standard cover, the quasi-coherent `F` becomes `tilde`-of-a-module
392	(via the local presentation and right-exactness of `tilde`), and the section-restriction there is an
393	`IsLocalizedModule` precisely by these lemmas.
394	
395	`tilde_section_isLocalizedModule` is the pure `tilde` case; the `[IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]` corollary
396	`section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` transports it across the canonical isomorphism
397	`F ≅ Γ(F)^~`.  Project-local because Mathlib states `toOpen` (the localization of `M` itself into
398	`Γ(D(f), M^~)`) but not the *section-restriction* form `Γ(⊤, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` that the keystone and
399	the `fromTildeΓ` counit consume. -/
400	
401	section LocalModel
402	
403	/-- **Route B local model (pure `tilde` case).**  For an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map
404	`Γ(Spec R, M^~) → Γ(D(f), M^~)` of the associated sheaf exhibits its target as the localization of
405	its source at the powers of `f`: `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` of that restriction.  This is the
406	section-restriction form of Mathlib's `tilde.toOpen` localization instance (which localizes `M`
407	itself, not the global sections `Γ(⊤, M^~)`), obtained by transporting along the global-sections
408	isomorphism `tilde.isoTop`.  Project-local; the load-bearing local model of the keystone
409	`qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`. -/
410	lemma tilde_section_isLocalizedModule (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
411	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
412	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
413	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
414	  have key := tilde.toOpen_res M ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) (homOfLE le_top)
415	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is an isomorphism; view it as a linear equivalence `eTop : M ≃ₗ Γ(⊤, M^~)`
416	  set eTop : M ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (tilde.toOpen M ⊤)).toLinearEquiv with heTop
417	  -- the section-restriction equals `toOpen (D f) ∘ eTop⁻¹` as linear maps
418	  have hmap : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
419	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
420	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap := by
421	    apply LinearMap.ext
422	    intro x
423	    have hk := congrArg (fun (m : M ⟶ _) => m.hom (eTop.symm x)) key
424	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hk
425	    have heq : ⇑eTop = ⇑(tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by rw [heTop]; ext y; simp

431	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers f)
432	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom eTop.symm

434	/-- **Route B local model (counit-isomorphism case).**  If the tilde–Γ counit of an

436	section-restriction `Γ(Spec R, F) → Γ(D(f), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its

439	Project-local; this is the per-piece engine of the keystone `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule`: on

441	global presentation there), so this lemma supplies the `IsLocalizedModule` datum the span-cover
442	descent consumes. -/
443	lemma section_isLocalizedModule_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ (F : (Spec R).Modules)
444	    [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
445	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
446	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
447	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
448	  set M := moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F with hM
449	  set α : F ≅ tilde M := qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F with hα

451	  set β := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map α.hom) with hβ

453	  haveI : IsIso β := inferInstance
454	  haveI : IsIso (β.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) :=
455	    CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_app_of_isIso β _
456	  haveI : IsIso (β.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) :=
457	    CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_app_of_isIso β _
458	  -- the two `β`-components as linear equivalences
459	  set eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ :=
460	    (asIso (β.app (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv with heTop
461	  set eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ :=
462	    (asIso (β.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv with heDf
463	  -- the `tilde M` restriction localizes (the local model), conjugate it by `eTop` on the source
464	  haveI hbrick := tilde_section_isLocalizedModule M f
465	  set φ : _ →ₗ[R] _ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
466	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom ∘ₗ eTop.toLinearMap with hφ
467	  haveI hφloc : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) φ := by
468	    rw [hφ]
469	    exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers f)
470	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
471	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom eTop
472	  -- naturality of `β`, read at `.hom` level: `eDf ∘ ρ_F = ρ_{tilde M} ∘ eTop = φ`
473	  have hnat := β.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op

475	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x) = φ x := by
476	    intro x
477	    have hx := LinearMap.congr_fun (congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat) x
478	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at hx
479	    exact hx
480	  -- so `ρ_F = eDf⁻¹ ∘ φ`, hence localizes (post-compose a localization with a linear equiv)
481	  have hF : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
482	      (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
483	      = eDf.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ φ := by
484	    apply LinearMap.ext
485	    intro x
486	    change _ = eDf.symm (φ x)
487	    rw [← hnat' x, eDf.symm_apply_apply]
488	  rw [hF]
489	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers f) φ eDf.symm

501	    (P : F.Presentation) (f : R) :

503	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
504	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by

510	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B step B1: finite presentation cover
511	
512	`qcoh_finite_presentation_cover` (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1): for a quasi-coherent `F` on
513	`Spec R`, the local quasi-coherence datum (`QuasicoherentData`) refines to a *finite* standard
514	cover `D(g_j) ⊆ U_{φ(j)}` with `span{g_j} = ⊤`, each member `U_{φ(j)}` carrying a presentation of
515	`F.over U_{φ(j)}`.  This is the entry point of the keystone descent: per `g_j` the attached
516	presentation restricts (B2) and bridges (B3/B4) to a global presentation of the affine restriction,
517	which the `IsLocalizedModule` local model then consumes.
518	
519	`coversTop_iSup_eq_top` is the topological translation feeding `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`:
520	the cover-of-terminal-object condition of a `QuasicoherentData` on the opens-Grothendieck-topology
521	of a space is the honest `⨆ U_i = ⊤`. -/

523	section FinitePresentationCover
524	
525	/-- A family of opens that covers the terminal object of the opens-Grothendieck-topology of a space
526	has supremum `⊤`.  Project-local: translates the categorical `J.CoversTop` condition carried by a
527	`QuasicoherentData` into the lattice-theoretic `⨆ = ⊤` that `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`
528	consumes. -/
529	private lemma coversTop_iSup_eq_top {T : Type*} [TopologicalSpace T] {I : Type*}
530	    (Y : I → TopologicalSpace.Opens T)
531	    (hY : (Opens.grothendieckTopology T).CoversTop Y) :
532	    ⨆ i, Y i = ⊤ := by
533	  rw [eq_top_iff]
534	  intro x _
535	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
536	  obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hU⟩ := hY ⊤ x (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x)
537	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
538	  exact ⟨i, (leOfHom g) hU⟩
539	
540	/-- **Finite presentation cover from quasi-coherence (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1).**  For a
541	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, there is a quasi-coherence datum `q` (a cover `q.X` of
542	`Spec R` together with a presentation of `F.over (q.X i)` for each `i`) and a *finite* standard
543	refinement of it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `span (range g) = ⊤` and indices
544	`φ : Fin n → q.I` such that each basic open `D(g j)` lies inside the cover member `q.X (φ j)`.  The
545	presentation of `F.over (q.X (φ j))` carried by `q` is what steps B2–B4 restrict to `D(g j)`.
546	Project-local: packages Mathlib's `QuasicoherentData` (local generation) with the affine finite
547	basic-open refinement (`exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`) in the exact form the Route B keystone
548	descent consumes. -/
549	lemma qcoh_finite_presentation_cover (F : (Spec R).Modules)
550	    [hF : F.IsQuasicoherent] :
551	    ∃ (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} F)
552	      (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → q.I),
553	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g j) ≤ q.X (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range g) = ⊤ := by
554	  obtain ⟨q⟩ := hF.nonempty_quasicoherentData
555	  have htop : ⨆ i, q.X i = ⊤ := coversTop_iSup_eq_top q.X q.coversTop
556	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩ := exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover q.X htop
557	  exact ⟨q, n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩
558	
559	end FinitePresentationCover

561	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B keystone: degree-0/1 sheaf-axiom equalizer
562	
563	`qcoh_section_equalizer` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8, sheaf-axiom equalizer route) is the degree-`0/1`
564	{\v C}ech equalizer of a sheaf of modules `F` on `Spec R`, read off the sheaf condition for a finite
565	(in fact arbitrary) family `U` covering an open `W`:
566	`0 → Γ(W,F) → ∏ⱼ Γ(Uⱼ,F) → ∏ⱼₖ Γ(Uⱼ ⊓ Uₖ,F)` is exact at both non-zero terms.  It is the entry
567	point of the keystone kernel comparison: instantiated at `W = ⊤` and `W = D(f)` (with `Uⱼ = D(gⱼ)`
568	resp. `Uⱼ = D(f gⱼ)`) it exhibits `Γ(X,F)` and `Γ(D(f),F)` as the kernels of the two overlap
569	differentials, which the localisation comparison then matches.  Non-circular: only the sheaf
570	condition of `F` is used, never a section-localisation identity. -/
571	
572	section SectionEqualizer
573	
574	/-- Restriction of a section across a composite inclusion `A ≤ B ≤ C` equals the single restriction
575	along `A ≤ C` (presheaf functoriality in the thin opens category).  Project-local helper for the
576	degree-0/1 differential computation of `qcoh_section_equalizer`. -/
577	private lemma res_trans_apply (P : TopCat.Presheaf (ModuleCat R) (Spec R))
578	    {A B C : (Spec R).Opens} (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (s : P.obj (.op C)) :
579	    (P.map (homOfLE h1).op).hom ((P.map (homOfLE h2).op).hom s)
580	      = (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op).hom s := by
581	  rw [← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp]; rfl
582	
583	/-- **Degree-0/1 sheaf-axiom equalizer (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8).**  For a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules
584	`F`, an open `W`, and a family `U : ι → Opens` with `U i ≤ W` for all `i` and `W ≤ ⨆ U i`, the
585	augmented two-term {\v C}ech sequence
586	`0 → Γ(W,F) →[ρ] ∏ᵢ Γ(U i,F) →[δ] ∏_{i,k} Γ(U i ⊓ U k,F)` is exact: the restriction product `ρ` is
587	injective and its range is exactly the kernel of the overlap differential `δ` (the difference of the
588	two restriction-to-overlap maps).  This is the sheaf condition of `F` read in degrees `0` and `1`,
589	proved from `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext` (injectivity) and
590	`TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'` (gluing of a matching family).  Project-local: Mathlib packages
591	the sheaf condition as a categorical limit / unique-gluing statement, not in the explicit
592	`Function.Exact` form on the section modules that the keystone kernel comparison consumes. -/
593	theorem qcoh_section_equalizer (F : (Spec R).Modules) {ι : Type u}
594	    (W : (Spec R).Opens) (U : ι → (Spec R).Opens)
595	    (hUW : ∀ i, U i ≤ W) (hWU : W ≤ iSup U) :
596	    Function.Injective
597	        ((LinearMap.pi fun i =>
598	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom) :
599	          (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op W) →ₗ[R]
600	            (Π i, (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op (U i)))) ∧
601	      Function.Exact
602	        ((LinearMap.pi fun i =>
603	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom) :
604	          (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (.op W) →ₗ[R] _)
605	        ((LinearMap.pi fun p : ι × ι =>
606	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
607	              (homOfLE (inf_le_right : U p.1 ⊓ U p.2 ≤ U p.2)).op).hom ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj p.2
608	          - ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
609	              (homOfLE (inf_le_left : U p.1 ⊓ U p.2 ≤ U p.1)).op).hom ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj p.1)) := by
610	  set P := modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F with hP
611	  have hsheaf : P.presheaf.IsSheaf := P.2
612	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
613	  · -- injectivity: a section is determined by its restrictions to the cover (`section_ext`)
614	    intro s s' hss
615	    have hcomp : ∀ i, (P.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom s
616	        = (P.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hUW i)).op).hom s' := fun i => congrFun hss i
617	    apply hsheaf.section_ext
618	    intro x hx
619	    have hxU : x ∈ iSup U := hWU hx
620	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup] at hxU
621	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hxU
622	    exact ⟨U i, hUW i, hi, hcomp i⟩
623	  · -- exactness at the middle: `δ t = 0 ↔ t` glues to a section over `W`
624	    intro t
625	    constructor
626	    · intro ht
627	      have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible P.presheaf U t := by
628	        intro i j
629	        have hij := congrFun ht (i, j)
630	        simp only [LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sub_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
631	          LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.zero_apply, sub_eq_zero] at hij
632	        exact hij.symm
633	      obtain ⟨s, hs, _⟩ := P.existsUnique_gluing' U W (fun i => homOfLE (hUW i)) hWU t hcompat
634	      exact ⟨s, funext fun i => hs i⟩
635	    · rintro ⟨s, rfl⟩
636	      funext p
637	      simp only [LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sub_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
638	        LinearMap.proj_apply, Pi.zero_apply, sub_eq_zero]
639	      rw [res_trans_apply, res_trans_apply]
640	
641	end SectionEqualizer

643	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base-ring descent of `IsLocalizedModule`
644	
645	`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap` is the converse of Mathlib's
646	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars`: an `A`-linear map that is a localization at the powers of
647	`algebraMap R A f` (over the larger base `A`) is, viewed `R`-linearly, a localization at the powers
648	of `f` (over the smaller base `R`).  This is the base-ring descent the Route B keystone needs: the
649	per-tile localizations produced over `R_g = Localization.Away g` (the tile's base ring) must be read
650	as `R`-localizations at the powers of `f ∈ R` to feed the kernel comparison, which localizes the
651	`X`-cover equalizer at `powers f ⊆ R`.  Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the ascent
652	direction. -/
653	
654	section BaseRingDescent
655	
656	/-- **Base-ring descent of `IsLocalizedModule` (converse of `of_restrictScalars`).**  Let `A` be an
657	`R`-algebra, let `M N` be `A`-modules with the compatible `R`-module structures (`IsScalarTower`),
658	and let `φ : M →ₗ[A] N`.  If `φ` is a localization at the powers of `algebraMap R A f` *over `A`*,
659	then its `R`-linear restriction `φ.restrictScalars R` is a localization at the powers of `f` *over
660	`R`*.  Proved directly from the three defining clauses of `IsLocalizedModule`, using that
661	`(algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x = f ^ k • x` by the scalar tower.  Project-local: Mathlib has only the
662	ascent `IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars`, not this descent. -/
663	lemma isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap
664	    {R A M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
665	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
666	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N] (f : R) (φ : M →ₗ[A] N)
667	    (h : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R A f)) φ) :
668	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (φ.restrictScalars R) := by
669	  have hsmul : ∀ (k : ℕ) (n : N), (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • n = f ^ k • n := by
670	    intro k n; rw [← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
671	  have hsmulM : ∀ (k : ℕ) (m : M), (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • m = f ^ k • m := by
672	    intro k m; rw [← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
673	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
674	  · rintro ⟨s, k, rfl⟩
675	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
676	    have hb := h.map_units ⟨(algebraMap R A f) ^ k, k, rfl⟩
677	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hb
678	    convert hb using 1
679	    ext n
680	    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
681	    exact (hsmul k n).symm
682	  · intro y
683	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, hs⟩ := h.surj y
684	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
685	    simp only [] at hk
686	    refine ⟨⟨m, ⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
687	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs ⊢
688	    calc f ^ k • y = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • y := (hsmul k y).symm
689	      _ = (s : A) • y := by rw [hk]
690	      _ = φ m := hs
691	  · intro x₁ x₂ he
692	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h.exists_of_eq he
693	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
694	    simp only [] at hk
695	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
696	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs ⊢
697	    calc f ^ k • x₁ = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x₁ := (hsmulM k x₁).symm
698	      _ = (s : A) • x₁ := by rw [hk]
699	      _ = (s : A) • x₂ := hs
700	      _ = (algebraMap R A f) ^ k • x₂ := by rw [hk]
701	      _ = f ^ k • x₂ := hsmulM k x₂
702	
703	end BaseRingDescent

705	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B keystone: per-tile section localization
706	
707	`tile_section_localization` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8, the last keystone leaf) shows that for a
708	quasi-coherent `F` on `Spec R` and elements `f g : R` with the tile `F_{(g)}` globally presented, the
709	section-restriction `Γ(D(g), F) → Γ(D(gf), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its source
710	at the powers of `f`.  It is the per-tile localization datum the sheaf-axiom kernel comparison
711	consumes (`analogies/keystone-descent.md`).
712	
713	The naive recipe "the section comparison is the `restrict_obj` rfl" is UNSOUND: `restrict_obj` is rfl
714	only for the local-ring `SheafOfModules` section functor `Γ(M,-)`, whereas the localization lives in
715	the global-ring functor `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj`, which does NOT commute with restriction
716	definitionally.  Hence the honest base-ring descent: Sub-lemma A (opens identities,
717	`tile_image_opens_identities`) + Sub-lemma B (the load-bearing natural section comparison,
718	`tile_section_comparison`) + the DONE base-ring descent
719	`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`. -/
720	
721	section TileSectionLocalization
722	
723	open TopologicalSpace
724	
725	/-- The `R`-action of the global-ring section functor `modulesSpecToSheaf` on a section reduces to
726	the structure-sheaf scalar action of the restricted global-sections element.  This is the rfl bridge
727	between the `ModuleCat R`-level action (via restriction of scalars along `globalSectionsIso`) and
728	the genuine `Γ(W, 𝒪)`-module action of `F.val`.  Project-local: the entry point of the tile scalar
729	reconciliation. -/
730	lemma modulesSpecToSheaf_smul_eq (F : (Spec R).Modules) (W : (Spec R).Opens) (r : R)
731	    (x : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W)) :
732	    r • x = (((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (homOfLE (le_top : W ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
733	              ((StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R).hom.hom r)
734	            • (show F.val.obj (Opposite.op W) from x)) :=
735	  rfl
736	
737	/-- The module action on the affine tile `modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F` transports rfl-style to the
738	`F.val` structure-sheaf action via the two open-immersion `appIso` ring maps of the iterated
739	restriction.  Project-local: the second rfl bridge of the tile scalar reconciliation. -/
740	lemma modulesRestrictBasicOpen_smul_eq (F : (Spec R).Modules) (g : R)
741	    (c : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).ringCatSheaf.val.obj
742	          (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)))
743	    (m : (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F).val.obj
744	          (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens))) :
745	    c • m = (((specBasicOpen g).ι.appIso _).inv.hom
746	              (((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv.appIso _).inv.hom c))
747	            • (show F.val.obj (Opposite.op ((specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ
748	                ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
749	                  (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)))) from m) :=
750	  rfl
751

22	\section{Setup and motivation}
23	
24	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
25	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
26	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
27	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
28	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
29	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
30	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
31	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
32	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
33	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
34	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
35	
36	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
37	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
38	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
39	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
40	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
41	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
42	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
43	\[
44	  \begin{array}{ccc}
45	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
46	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
47	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
48	  \end{array}
49	\]
50	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
51	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
52	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
